meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't collaborate (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't collaborate. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Overcome Collaboration Hurdles (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Learn to debug with AI and solve collaboration roadblocks. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your dev projects.
content.answer_block: Collaboration stalls when codebases become opaque to team members. By leveraging AI-driven debugging, you can generate instant architectural context and automated fixes. Use Antigravity to map dependencies and Claude Code to execute precision refactors, turning individual confusion into clear, actionable code paths that keep your team moving forward.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the deadline looms, and you are trapped in a labyrinth of your own making. You reach out to a peer, but they can't make sense of your logic. The 'can't collaborate' wall is the silent killer of every student project, transforming potential MVPs into abandoned GitHub repositories.

But the era of the lonely, stuck developer is over. By integrating AI into your workflow, you aren't just fixing bugs; you are creating a universal translator for your code. We are going to turn your codebase into a transparent asset, using Antigravity to map the logic and Claude Code to bridge the gap between your intent and your implementation.

steps.title: Establish the Source of Truth
steps.description: Before fixing, you must map the current state. Antigravity helps visualize how your modules interact.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory and generate a high-level dependency map, highlighting where collaboration friction points exist based on module complexity.

steps.title: Isolate the Collaboration Block
steps.description: Identify the specific code segment causing the friction. Use AI to extract the logic into a standalone, testable unit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Extract the core logic of the authentication module into a modular structure that is easier for others to review and test.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'refactor auth logic into isolated, documented functions'

steps.title: Automate Documentation Synthesis
steps.description: If others can't read your code, they can't collaborate. Use AI to generate human-readable documentation directly from your logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md and inline JSDoc comments that explain the 'why' behind this specific business logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'generate comprehensive documentation for all exported functions'

steps.title: Implement AI-Assisted Peer Review
steps.description: Use Claude Code to simulate a senior developer review to catch issues before a human even looks at the PR.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software engineer. Perform a code review on the latest changes, focusing on readability and potential collaboration bottlenecks.

steps.title: Verify Through Synthetic Testing
steps.description: Ensure your fixes are robust by generating automated tests that prove the code handles edge cases gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for the newly refactored modules to ensure they remain stable during team collaboration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'generate Jest tests for all refactored modules'

pro_tips: Treat your commit messages as technical documentation for your future self and your collaborators.
Use Claude Code to generate 'ADRs' (Architecture Decision Records) whenever you change a major dependency.
Run your codebase through an AI linter before every merge to maintain consistent style guidelines across the team.
Leverage Antigravity to identify 'God Objects'—classes that do too much—and break them down before they become unmanageable.
Always include a 'How to Run' section in your AI-generated docs to lower the barrier for new contributors.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your current project, deploy your application to a staging environment and invite a peer to test the new documentation. Use the feedback to iterate on your AI prompts.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical leverage to turn any chaotic codebase into a streamlined, collaborative machine. Stop struggling with solo bottlenecks and start building at scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
